description: Create the deploy.sh script for automated server deployment of anti_stalker_pro, following the exact same pattern as teleexport/deploy.sh. Prompts for all 7 credentials with validation, installs system deps, creates Python venv, installs pip dependencies from requirements.txt, writes .env config, creates systemd service, and handles errors gracefully.
steps: Create /projects/sandbox/Export-bot/anti_stalker_pro/deploy.sh following the teleexport/deploy.sh pattern exactly. The script must: (1) Use set -eo pipefail and color-coded logging helpers (info/success/warn/error), (2) Check for sudo and detect REAL_USER/REAL_HOME, (3) Print a banner for Anti-Stalker Intelligence System, (4) Clean old installation: stop/disable/remove anti_stalker.service, remove old venv at ~/.anti_stalker/venv, remove old config at ~/.anti_stalker/config, remove old data sessions, (5) System checks: verify Ubuntu, check RAM >= 1GB, check disk >= 2GB free, (6) Install system deps via apt-get (software-properties-common, build-essential, curl), (7) Install Python 3.11 via deadsnakes PPA if not present, (8) Ensure pip for Python 3.11, (9) Create venv at ~/.anti_stalker/venv, (10) Install dependencies from the project's requirements.txt into the venv, (11) Prompt for ALL 7 credentials with validation: TELEGRAM_API_ID (numeric), TELEGRAM_API_HASH (32 hex chars), TELEGRAM_PHONE (starts with +, 10-15 digits), BOT_TOKEN (contains colon, format NNN:XXXXX), MY_TELEGRAM_ID (numeric), DASHBOARD_SECRET_KEY (min 16 chars, generate random if empty), TRACKING_REDIRECT_URL (starts with http), (12) Write ~/.anti_stalker/config/.env with all credentials and default values for intervals/thresholds/ports, (13) Create data directories (data/logs, data/backups, data/reports, data/sessions), (14) Create systemd service at /etc/systemd/system/anti_stalker.service that runs main.py with the venv Python, with EnvironmentFile pointing to the .env, (15) Enable service, print auth instructions for Telethon first-run, (16) Print service management commands (start/stop/status/logs/restart), (17) Error trap with cleanup function and line-number error reporting.
acceptance_criteria: deploy.sh follows the same structural pattern as teleexport/deploy.sh (banner, sudo check, clean, system checks, deps, Python, venv, credentials, config, systemd)
All 7 credentials are prompted with proper validation loops (while true; read; validate; break)
TELEGRAM_API_HASH validates as exactly 32 hex characters
TELEGRAM_PHONE validates as starting with + and 10-15 digit format
BOT_TOKEN validates as containing colon in format NUMBER:HASH
DASHBOARD_SECRET_KEY has minimum 16 chars or auto-generates a random one
TRACKING_REDIRECT_URL validates as starting with http
The .env file is written with all credentials plus default config values
Systemd service file correctly references venv Python and project main.py
Script uses set -eo pipefail and has error trap with line numbers
verification: cd /projects/sandbox/Export-bot/anti_stalker_pro && grep -c 'read -rp' deploy.sh | xargs test 7 -le && echo 'All 7 prompts present'
cd /projects/sandbox/Export-bot/anti_stalker_pro && grep 'set -eo pipefail' deploy.sh && echo 'Error handling present'
cd /projects/sandbox/Export-bot/anti_stalker_pro && grep 'systemctl' deploy.sh && echo 'Systemd setup present'

cd /projects/sandbox/Export-bot/anti_stalker_pro && grep 'TELEGRAM_API_ID\|TELEGRAM_API_HASH\|TELEGRAM_PHONE\|BOT_TOKEN\|MY_TELEGRAM_ID\|DASHBOARD_SECRET_KEY\|TRACKING_REDIRECT_URL' deploy.sh | wc -l | xargs test 7 -le && echo 'All credentials handled'
findings: deploy.sh created following exact teleexport/deploy.sh pattern. All 7 credential prompts with while-true validation loops, bash -n syntax check passes, all verification checks pass. Script is chmod +x.
